cells.cell_text: D. jangii + D. jonesii 1 & 2 + D. coonoorensis

page.content: The table presents data on various genera within the Node category, specifically detailing the Ancestral area, Mean (my), Confidence Interval (CI), and Effective Sample Size (ESS) for each entry. The first entry under Genus Digitipes in WG for the ancestral area SWG has a mean of 86, with a CI of 79–91 and an ESS of 2102. The second entry, except O. ruficeps, is also from SWG, with a mean of 78, CI of 65–86, and ESS of 975. The third entry lists D. jangii + D. jonesii 1 & 2 + D. coonoorensis, spanning SWG & CWG, having a mean of 61, a CI of 48–75, and an ESS of 584. The fourth entry is D. jangii, from the CWG area, with a mean of 46, CI of 32–60 and ESS of 661. The fifth entry, D. barnabasi 1 – D. barnabasi 2, covers SWG & NWG, with a mean of 58, CI of 43–71, and ESS of 435. D. barnabasi 1, located in SWG, shows a mean of 50, a CI of 35–63, and an ESS of 538, while D. barnabasi 2, from NWG, has a mean of 42, CI of 28–55, and an ESS of 565. D. jonesii appears twice, first with a mean of 21, CI of 12–32, ESS of 791, and again with a mean of 42, CI of 30–54, and ESS of 726. D. coonoorensis, spanning SWG & Nilgiris, has a mean of 35, a CI of 24–48, and ESS of 814. Finally, O. ruficeps, from SWG, has a mean of 43, CI of 25–60, and an ESS of 750.
